title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Mariana Trench is the deepest part of the world’s oceans, (0) located in the western Pacific East of the Philippines. (9) Although it remains one of the most hostile environments on Earth, recent expeditions have found life thriving in its dark depths. (10) Such is the extreme pressure at the bottom that it would crush a regular submarine in a matter of seconds. (11) Provided that specialized equipment is used, humans can safely descend to explore this vast, uncharted territory of the seafloor. (12) Not only do these deep-sea vents provide heat, but they also release chemicals that sustain unique biological communities without sunlight. (13) Regardless of the darkness, these organisms have adapted to survive using chemosynthesis instead of the more common photosynthesis. (14) Not until the historic dive of the Trieste in 1960 did we have any real understanding of what lay at the bottom. (15) In spite of the incredible depth, traces of plastic pollution have unfortunately been discovered even in these remote areas. (16) Whether we can protect these fragile ecosystems from human impact is a growing concern for marine biologists. This hidden world continues to challenge our understanding of the limits of life on our planet.

example.text: The Mariana Trench is the deepest part of the world’s oceans, (0) located in the western Pacific.
example.explanation: Past participle used to describe the position or site.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the clause.
Conjunction used to express a condition.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed phrase 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did we have').
Part of the prepositional linker 'In spite of'.
Conjunction introducing a doubt between possibilities.
